verdict.summary: PR hardens _salted_labels in scripts/dashboard-gen.py to reject a non-string, blank, or non-printable PROVENANCE_SALT before hashing, and adds two self-test assertions. The change strengthens the existing fail-closed posture: account_hash (model-health.py:249) only rejects falsy salts, so the new guard additionally catches truthy-but-degenerate salts ('   ', 'salt\nvalue') that would otherwise produce a label. It does NOT weaken any trust check or diverge the shared hasher — valid salts still yield the identical sha256(handle:salt)[:16], and a bad configured salt now dies (fail-closed) rather than deploying a degenerate row. The two added self-tests are non-vacuous: (1) the 16-lowercase-hex regex assertion flips red if the label format regresses; (2) the invalid-salts loop would append False for '   ' and 'salt\nvalue' if the new guard were removed (account_hash accepts them), so the assertion [True,True,True] genuinely exercises the new rejection path. None is correctly caught since the isinstance guard raises DashboardError, matching the loop's except clause. re is imported (line 14); DashboardError subclasses RuntimeError; check() uses ==. No token/PII exposure, no workflow/permission changes, no injection content in the diff.
